title: Lens-to-Body Communication Error
shortDescription: The camera cannot communicate properly with the attached lens.
fullDescription:
Err 01 is one of the most common Canon errors and indicates a communication breakdown between the camera body and the lens. The electrical contacts on either the lens mount or camera body are dirty, damaged, or not making proper contact.

This error frequently appears when using third-party lenses, lens adapters, or after the camera or lens has been exposed to moisture or dust. It can also occur with genuine Canon lenses that have worn contact pins.

In most cases, cleaning the lens and body contacts resolves the issue. If the error persists with multiple lenses, the camera body contacts may need professional repair.

causes.cause: Dirty or corroded electrical contacts on lens or body
Lens not mounted securely on the camera body

symptoms: Err 01 displayed on the top LCD or rear screen
Camera cannot autofocus
Aperture stuck wide open or at minimum
Shutter does not fire
Error appears intermittently during shooting

fixSteps.title: Clean the lens contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lens from the camera. Using a clean microfiber cloth or a pencil eraser, gently wipe the gold electrical contacts on the rear of the lens. Wipe in one direction to avoid pushing debris into the contacts.

fixSteps.title: Clean the camera body contacts
fixSteps.instruction: With the lens removed, locate the electrical contact pins inside the camera lens mount. Gently clean them with a microfiber cloth dampened with isopropyl alcohol. Allow to dry completely before reattaching the lens.

fixSteps.title: Remount the lens securely
fixSteps.instruction: Align the red or white dot on the lens with the corresponding mark on the camera body. Mount the lens and turn clockwise until it clicks firmly into place. Ensure no wobble.

fixSteps.title: Test with a different lens
fixSteps.instruction: If available, mount a different Canon lens to determine if the issue is with the lens or the camera body. If the error only occurs with one lens, that lens needs service.

fixSteps.title: Update third-party lens firmware
fixSteps.instruction: If using a Sigma, Tamron, or other third-party lens, check the manufacturer's website for firmware updates. Sigma lenses can be updated via the Sigma USB Dock.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning contacts does not resolve the issue and the error occurs with multiple lenses, the camera body contact assembly may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 01 lens communication error fix

faq.q: Why does Err 01 only appear with my third-party lens?
faq.a: Third-party lenses use reverse-engineered communication protocols. Firmware updates from the lens manufacturer or Canon body firmware updates can cause incompatibilities. Check for lens firmware updates.

faq.q: Can I use contact cleaner spray on the lens contacts?
faq.a: Use isopropyl alcohol on a microfiber cloth instead. Spray cleaners can leave residue or force liquid into the lens or camera body.

faq.q: Does Err 01 mean my lens is broken?
faq.a: Not necessarily. In most cases it is a contact issue that cleaning resolves. Only if the error persists after thorough cleaning with multiple lenses should you suspect hardware failure.

title: Memory Card Read/Write Error
shortDescription: The camera cannot read from or write to the inserted memory card.
fullDescription:
Err 02 indicates that the camera is unable to communicate with the memory card. This can be caused by a corrupted card, dirty contacts, a card that is not compatible with the camera, or a faulty card reader slot in the camera body.

This error often appears after removing the card while the camera was writing data, using a card formatted in a different device, or when the card has reached the end of its lifespan. High-speed UHS-II cards in cameras that only support UHS-I can also trigger this error.

Formatting the card in the camera usually resolves the issue. Always back up your images before formatting.
symptoms: Err 02 displayed on screen
Camera cannot take photos or record video
Images appear corrupted or missing
Card not recognized when inserted
Slow write speeds before error occurs

fixSteps.title: Remove and reinsert the memory card
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera, open the card slot door, remove the memory card, wait 10 seconds, and reinsert it firmly until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean the card contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Use a clean, dry microfiber cloth or a pencil eraser to gently clean the gold contacts on the memory card. Blow out the card slot with compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Format the card in-camera
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup (wrench icon) > Format Card. Select Low Level Format for a thorough format. WARNING: This erases all data on the card. Back up photos first if possible.

fixSteps.title: Try a different memory card
fixSteps.instruction: Insert a known-good memory card to determine if the issue is with the card or the camera. Use a card from a reputable brand (SanDisk, Sony, Lexar) rated for your camera.

fixSteps.title: Recover data from corrupted card
fixSteps.instruction: If you need to recover images, use a card reader and data recovery software such as PhotoRec, Recuva, or Canon's own EOS Utility before formatting the card.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs with multiple known-good cards, the camera's card slot may need repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 02 memory card error fix

faq.q: Will formatting the card erase all my photos?
faq.a: Yes. Always try to back up photos to a computer using a card reader before formatting. Data recovery software may help recover files from a corrupted card.

faq.q: What memory card should I use with my Canon camera?
faq.a: Use UHS-I or UHS-II SD cards from reputable brands. For R5/R3, CFexpress Type B cards are recommended for video. Check your camera manual for supported card types.

faq.q: Why does my camera say Err 02 with a brand new card?
faq.a: New cards should be formatted in the camera before first use. Some high-capacity or high-speed cards may not be compatible with older camera models.

title: Memory Card Full or Write Error
shortDescription: The memory card is full or the camera cannot save images to it.
fullDescription:
Err 04 means the camera cannot save images to the memory card, either because the card is full or because there is a write error. This can also appear if the card has been write-protected using the physical lock switch on the side of an SD card.

In some cases, the card may show available space on a computer but still trigger this error on the camera. This typically indicates filesystem fragmentation or a mismatch between the card's format and what the camera expects.

Formatting the card in the camera after backing up your files is the most reliable solution.

causes.cause: SD card write-protect switch is locked
Card formatted in wrong filesystem (exFAT vs FAT32)

symptoms: Err 04 displayed on screen
Camera stops recording mid-video
Shutter fires but image is not saved
Card shows FuLL on top LCD
Intermittent save failures during burst shooting

fixSteps.title: Check the card write-protect switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the SD card and check the small sliding switch on the left side. Ensure it is in the unlocked (up) position. Reinsert the card.

fixSteps.title: Delete unwanted images or transfer files
fixSteps.instruction: Review and delete unneeded photos/videos on the camera, or connect to a computer and transfer files to free up space.

fixSteps.title: Format the card in-camera
fixSteps.instruction: After backing up important files, go to Menu > Setup > Format Card. This clears fragmentation and resets the filesystem to the camera's preferred format.

fixSteps.title: Replace the memory card
fixSteps.instruction: If the card consistently triggers Err 04 even after formatting, replace it with a new card. SD cards have a limited number of write cycles.

whenToCallPro: This error is almost always user-serviceable. Professional help is only needed if the camera's card slot is physically damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 04 card full error fix

faq.q: Where is the write-protect switch on an SD card?
faq.a: It is a small sliding switch on the left side of the SD card. When slid down toward the contacts, the card is locked and cannot be written to.

faq.q: Why does Err 04 appear when my card is not full?
faq.a: The card filesystem may be fragmented or formatted in a way the camera does not support. Format the card in the camera to resolve this.

title: Built-in Flash Malfunction
shortDescription: The camera's built-in flash unit could not fire or retract properly.
fullDescription:
Err 05 occurs when the camera's pop-up flash fails to deploy, retract, or charge. This error is specific to cameras with built-in flash units and does not apply to external speedlites.

The most common cause is the flash being physically stuck due to dirt, debris, or impact damage. The flash mechanism uses a small spring-loaded hinge that can become jammed or broken over time.

Gently assisting the flash to pop up or retract while powering the camera on and off usually resolves the issue. Avoid forcing the flash as this can break the hinge mechanism.

causes.cause: Flash unit physically stuck or jammed
Dirt or debris in the flash hinge mechanism

symptoms: Err 05 displayed on screen
Flash does not pop up when triggered
Flash pops up but does not fire
Flash stuck in the up or down position
Clicking sound from flash area without deployment

fixSteps.title: Gently assist the flash
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the camera off. Gently try to pop the flash up with your finger using light pressure on the flash button. Do not force it. Turn the camera back on and try the flash button.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flash hinge area
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow out any dust or debris from around the flash hinge at the top of the camera. A soft brush can help dislodge particles.

fixSteps.title: Reset the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery and memory card. Wait 30 seconds. Reinsert both and power on the camera. Try the flash again.

fixSteps.title: Use an external flash instead
fixSteps.instruction: If the built-in flash cannot be repaired, mount an external Canon Speedlite on the hot shoe as a workaround. The camera will use the external flash instead.

whenToCallPro: If the flash mechanism is physically broken or the flash tube has burned out, professional repair is needed. The flash capacitor stores dangerous voltage and should not be opened by non-professionals.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 05 flash error fix

faq.q: Does Err 05 affect external speedlites?
faq.a: No, Err 05 is specific to the camera's built-in pop-up flash. External speedlites have their own error codes.

faq.q: Is it dangerous to repair the flash myself?
faq.a: Yes, the flash capacitor stores high voltage even when the camera is off. Do not attempt to disassemble the flash unit. Let a professional handle internal flash repairs.

title: Self-Cleaning Sensor Malfunction
shortDescription: The camera's built-in sensor cleaning system has failed to operate.
fullDescription:
Err 06 indicates that the camera's automatic sensor cleaning mechanism could not complete its operation. Canon cameras use an ultrasonic vibration system on the low-pass filter in front of the sensor to shake off dust particles when the camera is turned on or off.

This error is relatively rare and usually occurs when the sensor cleaning unit's piezoelectric element is damaged or when the sensor assembly has shifted slightly due to impact. It does not mean the image sensor itself is damaged.

In most cases, turning the camera off and on again resolves this error. If it persists, the self-cleaning unit may need repair, but the camera can still be used normally by disabling automatic sensor cleaning.

causes.cause: Temporary glitch in self-cleaning startup sequence
Piezoelectric cleaning element worn or damaged
Sensor assembly shifted after impact or drop

symptoms: Err 06 displayed on screen during power-on or power-off
Sensor cleaning animation does not appear at startup
Dust spots appear more frequently on images
Camera takes longer to power on

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the camera off, remove the battery, wait 30 seconds, reinsert the battery, and turn the camera on. The self-cleaning system should reinitialize.

fixSteps.title: Manually trigger sensor cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Sensor Cleaning > Clean Now. This forces the cleaning cycle. If it completes without error, the issue was temporary.

fixSteps.title: Disable automatic sensor cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, go to Menu > Setup > Sensor Cleaning and disable automatic cleaning on startup/shutdown. This allows normal camera use while bypassing the faulty unit.

fixSteps.title: Manual sensor cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Use a rocket blower to remove loose dust from the sensor. For stubborn spots, use a sensor cleaning swab with cleaning solution appropriate for your sensor size (APS-C or full-frame).

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and you notice increasing dust on images despite manual cleaning, the self-cleaning unit may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 06 sensor cleaning error fix

faq.q: Can I still use my camera with Err 06?
faq.a: Yes. Disable automatic sensor cleaning in the menu and the camera will function normally. You will just need to clean the sensor manually more often.

faq.q: Does Err 06 mean my sensor is damaged?
faq.a: No. Err 06 only relates to the ultrasonic cleaning mechanism on the low-pass filter, not the image sensor itself.

title: File System or Internal Storage Error
shortDescription: An internal file system error has occurred preventing the camera from saving or reading files.
fullDescription:
Err 10 indicates a problem with the camera's internal file management system. This can be caused by corrupted image numbering, a full DCIM folder structure, or conflicts between the camera's file database and what is actually stored on the card.

This error most commonly appears when image numbers have reached the maximum (9999) within a folder, when files have been manually renamed or moved on a computer, or when a card that was used in a different camera is inserted without formatting.

Resetting the file numbering or formatting the card in-camera typically resolves this issue.

causes.cause: Corrupted file structure from computer editing
Card used in different camera without formatting

symptoms: Err 10 displayed on screen
Camera cannot create new folders
Images not appearing in playback despite successful capture
File numbering stuck or jumping erratically

fixSteps.title: Reset file numbering
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > File Numbering and select Auto Reset. This resets the image counter and creates a new folder on the card.

fixSteps.title: Format the memory card
fixSteps.instruction: Back up all images to a computer first. Then go to Menu > Setup > Format Card and perform a low-level format. This rebuilds the entire file structure.

fixSteps.title: Reset all camera settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings. This returns file numbering and all other settings to factory defaults without affecting firmware.

fixSteps.title: Update camera firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Download the latest firmware from Canon's support website. Copy to a formatted card, insert into camera, and follow the firmware update procedure.

whenToCallPro: If formatting and resetting do not resolve the error, the camera may need a firmware reinstall or service center diagnostics.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 10 file error fix

faq.q: Will resetting file numbering delete my photos?
faq.a: No. Resetting file numbering only changes how future files are named. Existing photos remain on the card.

faq.q: How do I prevent Err 10?
faq.a: Always format cards in the camera rather than on a computer. Avoid renaming files directly on the card. Use Auto Reset file numbering.

title: Mechanical Shutter or Mirror Malfunction
shortDescription: The camera's mechanical shutter or mirror mechanism is stuck or malfunctioning.
fullDescription:
Err 20 is a serious mechanical error indicating that the shutter mechanism or reflex mirror (on DSLR models) is unable to complete its movement cycle. The shutter blades may be stuck, the mirror may be jammed in the up or down position, or the shutter drive motor has failed.

This error can occur after heavy use (shutter mechanisms have a rated lifespan), after a drop or impact, or when debris enters the mirror box. In cold weather, lubricant can thicken and slow the mechanism enough to trigger this error.

While power cycling sometimes resolves a temporary jam, Err 20 often indicates the need for professional shutter replacement, especially on cameras that have exceeded their rated shutter count.

causes.cause: Shutter mechanism worn beyond rated lifespan
Debris in the mirror box or shutter cavity

symptoms: Err 20 displayed on screen
Shutter does not fire or fires irregularly
Mirror stuck in up position causing black viewfinder
Unusual clicking or grinding sounds from camera body
Intermittent blank or partially exposed images

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera, remove the battery for 60 seconds, reinsert it, and turn the camera back on. This may reset a temporarily jammed mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Switch to electronic shutter
fixSteps.instruction: If your camera supports it (mirrorless models), switch to electronic shutter mode in Menu > Shooting > Shutter Mode. This bypasses the mechanical shutter entirely.

fixSteps.title: Warm the camera in cold conditions
fixSteps.instruction: If shooting in cold weather, bring the camera to a warm environment and let it warm up for 15-20 minutes. Cold can thicken lubricants and cause temporary jams.

fixSteps.title: Check shutter count
fixSteps.instruction: Use a tool like ShutterCount (Mac) or EOSInfo (Windows) to check your camera's shutter actuation count. Compare to the rated lifespan (typically 150,000-500,000 depending on model).

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not resolve Err 20, professional repair is almost always required. Shutter replacement is a precision operation that should not be attempted at home.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 20 shutter malfunction fix

faq.q: How many shots can my Canon shutter handle?
faq.a: Entry-level models are rated for ~100,000-150,000 actuations, mid-range for ~200,000, and professional bodies for 300,000-500,000. Many cameras exceed their ratings.

faq.q: Can I replace the shutter myself?
faq.a: Shutter replacement requires precise alignment and specialized tools. It is strongly recommended to have this done by a Canon service center or qualified repair technician.

faq.q: Does using electronic shutter avoid Err 20?
faq.a: Yes, on mirrorless cameras the electronic shutter bypasses the mechanical shutter entirely, which can serve as a workaround while awaiting repair.

title: Shutter Malfunction — Cannot Complete Cycle
shortDescription: The shutter mechanism cannot complete its opening or closing cycle.
fullDescription:
Err 30 is closely related to Err 20 and specifically indicates that the shutter could not complete its full travel. While Err 20 often means the shutter is stuck, Err 30 indicates partial movement — the shutter started but could not finish opening or closing.

This is typically caused by a worn shutter mechanism that is nearing end of life, a foreign object obstructing shutter blade travel, or a weak shutter drive motor. It can also occur after a drop if the shutter rail has been bent.

Unlike Err 20, which can sometimes be resolved by power cycling, Err 30 almost always requires professional shutter replacement.

causes.cause: Shutter blades unable to complete travel
Shutter mechanism worn beyond rated lifespan
Foreign debris obstructing shutter blade path
Bent shutter rail from impact damage

symptoms: Err 30 displayed on screen
Partially exposed or banded images
Shutter sounds abnormal — sluggish or incomplete
Camera locks up after attempted shot
Viewfinder alternates between black and normal

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera, remove the battery for 60 seconds, then reinsert and power on. Attempt a test shot.

fixSteps.title: Try electronic shutter mode
fixSteps.instruction: On mirrorless Canon cameras, switch to electronic shutter in Menu > Shooting > Shutter Mode to bypass the mechanical shutter.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: With the lens removed, set the camera to sensor cleaning mode (Menu > Setup > Sensor Cleaning > Clean Manually) to lock the mirror up (DSLR). Inspect the shutter blades with a flashlight for visible debris. Do NOT touch the shutter blades.

fixSteps.title: Send for professional repair
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Canon support or an authorized service center. Provide your camera's serial number, shutter count if known, and describe the error. Expect shutter unit replacement.

whenToCallPro: Err 30 almost always requires professional repair. Do not attempt to manually move shutter blades as this can cause further damage.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 30 shutter error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between Err 20 and Err 30?
faq.a: Err 20 indicates the shutter or mirror is stuck and cannot start moving. Err 30 indicates it started but could not complete its cycle. Both typically require shutter replacement.

faq.q: Is Err 30 covered under warranty?
faq.a: If within the warranty period and not caused by physical damage, Canon may cover the repair. Contact Canon support with your purchase proof.

title: Power Supply Malfunction
shortDescription: The camera has detected a power supply issue with the battery or power adapter.
fullDescription:
Err 40 indicates that the camera's power management system has detected an issue with the power supply. This could be a failing battery, a problem with the battery grip connections, use of a non-genuine battery, or an issue with an AC power adapter.

Third-party batteries are a common cause of this error, as they may not properly communicate with Canon's power management system. Temperature extremes can also cause batteries to underperform and trigger this error.

Trying a different battery or using a genuine Canon battery is the quickest way to diagnose this issue.

causes.cause: Dirty battery contacts in the camera or battery grip

symptoms: Err 40 displayed on screen
Camera shuts down unexpectedly
Battery indicator shows incorrect charge level
Camera refuses to power on consistently
Error appears during high-power operations (flash, video, burst)

fixSteps.title: Charge or replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery and charge it fully using a genuine Canon charger. If fully charged and the error persists, try a different battery.

fixSteps.title: Clean battery contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the battery contacts on both the battery and inside the camera's battery compartment using a dry microfiber cloth or a pencil eraser.

fixSteps.title: Try a genuine Canon battery
fixSteps.instruction: If using a third-party battery, try a genuine Canon LP-E6NH or the battery specified for your model. Third-party batteries may not communicate properly.

fixSteps.title: Check battery grip connections
fixSteps.instruction: If using a battery grip, remove it and try the camera with just the body battery. Clean the grip's electrical contacts. Ensure the grip's battery tray is properly inserted.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists with a genuine fully-charged Canon battery and no battery grip, the camera's internal power board may need service.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 40 power supply error fix

faq.q: Can I use third-party batteries in my Canon camera?
faq.a: While many third-party batteries work fine, some may trigger Err 40 due to missing or incorrect communication chips. Genuine Canon batteries are recommended for reliability.

faq.q: Why does Err 40 only appear during video recording?
faq.a: Video recording draws significantly more power than still photography. A battery that appears to have charge may not be able to sustain the higher power draw, triggering the error.

title: General Electronic Control Error
shortDescription: An unspecified electronic control error has occurred within the camera.
fullDescription:
Err 50 is a general-purpose error code indicating that an electronic component or control system in the camera has malfunctioned. It can be triggered by a variety of internal issues including problems with the image processing engine, the main circuit board, or communication between internal components.

This error is often intermittent and may appear once and never return, or it may become persistent. A single occurrence after a power surge, static discharge, or firmware glitch is usually nothing to worry about.

Persistent Err 50 typically indicates a failing main board or image processor, which requires professional repair.

causes.cause: Temporary electronic glitch or static discharge

symptoms: Err 50 displayed on screen
Camera freezes and becomes unresponsive
Random shutdowns during use
Display shows garbled or distorted images
Camera resets settings unexpectedly

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera, remove the battery and memory card, wait 60 seconds, then reinsert both and power on. This clears temporary electronic states.

fixSteps.title: Let the camera cool down
fixSteps.instruction: If the camera has been shooting video or used in hot conditions, turn it off and let it cool for 15-20 minutes. Overheating can cause intermittent Err 50.

fixSteps.title: Reset all camera settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings. This returns all electronic settings to defaults and can resolve firmware-level glitches.

fixSteps.title: Update or reinstall firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Download the latest firmware from Canon's support site. Format a card, copy the firmware file to it, insert into the camera, and follow the update procedure.

whenToCallPro: If Err 50 occurs repeatedly after power cycling, settings reset, and firmware update, the main circuit board likely needs repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 50 electronic error fix

faq.q: Should I worry about a single Err 50 occurrence?
faq.a: A single occurrence is usually a temporary glitch caused by static discharge or a brief electronic hiccup. If it does not recur, there is no cause for concern.

faq.q: Can Err 50 be caused by a memory card?
faq.a: Indirectly, yes. A malfunctioning card can cause the camera's electronics to behave unexpectedly. Try a different card if the error persists.

title: General Malfunction — Non-Specific Hardware Error
shortDescription: A general non-specific hardware malfunction has been detected.
fullDescription:
Err 60 is a catch-all error code for hardware malfunctions that do not fall under more specific error categories. It indicates that the camera detected something abnormal during operation but cannot identify the specific component.

This error may be triggered by issues with the image stabilization system, the autofocus module, the display panel, or other internal subsystems. It can also appear when multiple minor issues compound.

Because Err 60 is non-specific, diagnosis involves systematic elimination — trying different lenses, cards, and batteries to isolate the problem.
symptoms: Err 60 displayed on screen
Camera freezes or becomes unresponsive
Inconsistent behavior across different features
Error appears in specific modes but not others

fixSteps.title: Full power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera, remove battery, lens, and memory card. Wait 2 minutes. Reattach the lens, insert battery and card, and power on.

fixSteps.title: Test with different accessories
fixSteps.instruction: Try a different lens, memory card, and battery one at a time to isolate the issue. Note which combination triggers the error.

fixSteps.title: Reset all settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings to eliminate any software configuration issue.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check Canon's website for firmware updates for your specific model. Firmware updates can resolve bugs that cause Err 60.

whenToCallPro: If Err 60 persists after trying different accessories and resetting settings, professional diagnostics are needed to identify the specific failing component.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 60 hardware error fix

faq.q: Why is Err 60 so vague?
faq.a: Err 60 is a catch-all for hardware issues that do not have dedicated error codes. Canon service centers have diagnostic tools that can pinpoint the exact component.

faq.q: Is Err 60 common?
faq.a: It is relatively uncommon. Single occurrences are usually temporary glitches. Repeated Err 60 warrants professional inspection.

title: Image Processing or DIGIC Processor Error
shortDescription: The camera's image processing engine (DIGIC) has encountered a critical error.
fullDescription:
Err 70 indicates a malfunction in the camera's DIGIC image processing engine. This processor handles image data conversion, noise reduction, autofocus calculations, and many other core functions.

The error can be caused by processor overheating during extended video recording, corrupted firmware, or a hardware failure of the DIGIC chip itself. On mirrorless cameras with in-body image stabilization, sensor-shift IBIS faults can also trigger Err 70.

A single occurrence after extended use in hot conditions is usually thermal-related and resolves after cooling. Persistent errors indicate a serious hardware problem.

causes.cause: Corrupted firmware or interrupted firmware update

symptoms: Err 70 displayed on screen
Camera locks up completely
Images show processing artifacts or corruption
Camera overheating warning before error
Autofocus stops working entirely

fixSteps.title: Cool down the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the camera immediately. Remove the battery and let the camera cool in a shaded, ventilated area for at least 20 minutes. Do not put it in a refrigerator.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle with fresh battery
fixSteps.instruction: After cooling, insert a fully charged battery and power on. If the error does not reappear, it was thermal-related.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Download your camera's current firmware version from Canon.com and reinstall it. This can repair corrupted firmware files even if the version number is the same.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings. If the camera is too unresponsive, try holding the SET button while powering on (check your model's manual for the specific reset combination).

whenToCallPro: If Err 70 persists after cooling, firmware reinstall, and factory reset, the DIGIC processor or main board likely needs replacement. This requires professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 70 image processing error fix

faq.q: Is the EOS R5 known for Err 70 during video?
faq.a: The EOS R5 is known to overheat during extended 8K or 4K HQ video recording, which can trigger Err 70. Firmware updates have improved thermal management, but there are physical limits.

faq.q: Can I prevent Err 70 from overheating?
faq.a: Shoot in shorter clips, use an external fan or heatsink accessory, avoid direct sunlight on the camera body, and disable image stabilization when on a tripod.

title: Electronic Control or Communication Anomaly
shortDescription: An anomaly in the camera's electronic control or communication system has been detected.
fullDescription:
Err 80 indicates an issue with the camera's internal communication bus — the electronic pathways that connect the processor, sensors, memory, and other components. This error is relatively rare and often appears after physical damage, liquid exposure, or a severe static discharge.

Unlike Err 50 (which is a processing issue), Err 80 specifically relates to communication between components. If one module cannot send or receive data correctly, the camera halts operation to prevent data corruption.

This error is almost always a hardware issue that requires professional repair.

causes.cause: Static discharge damaging internal bus connections
Liquid or moisture damage to circuit board

symptoms: Err 80 displayed on screen
Camera completely unresponsive
Random feature failures (AF, metering, display)
Camera powers on but cannot take photos
Error appears immediately at startup

fixSteps.title: Full power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove battery and all accessories (lens, card, grip). Wait 5 minutes. Reassemble and power on.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the battery compartment, card slot, and lens mount for any signs of moisture or corrosion. If wet, place the camera in a sealed container with silica gel packets for 24-48 hours.

fixSteps.title: Reset camera settings
fixSteps.instruction: If the camera is responsive enough, reset all settings to factory defaults via Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings.

fixSteps.title: Contact Canon service
fixSteps.instruction: Err 80 typically requires professional diagnosis. Contact Canon support or an authorized service center. Describe any recent drops, water exposure, or unusual events.

whenToCallPro: Err 80 almost always requires professional repair. If power cycling does not resolve it, do not delay sending it for service.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera Err 80 electronic error fix

faq.q: Is Err 80 a death sentence for my camera?
faq.a: Not necessarily. While it is a serious error, Canon service centers can often replace the affected circuit board and restore full functionality. The cost depends on the model and extent of damage.

faq.q: Can moisture cause Err 80 even in weather-sealed cameras?
faq.a: Yes. Weather sealing reduces but does not eliminate the risk of moisture intrusion, especially through the lens mount during lens changes in wet conditions.

title: General Unspecified Error
shortDescription: A general unspecified error has occurred that could involve any camera subsystem.
fullDescription:
Err 99 is Canon's most infamous error code — a catch-all that can be triggered by virtually any problem the camera detects but cannot classify into a more specific category. It has been reported on nearly every Canon DSLR and mirrorless camera.

The causes range from trivial (dirty lens contacts, bad battery) to serious (failing main board, damaged shutter). Because Err 99 is so non-specific, troubleshooting involves systematically testing each component.

The good news is that in the majority of cases, Err 99 is caused by lens contact issues, memory card problems, or battery issues — all of which are easily fixed. Only about 10-15% of Err 99 cases require professional repair.
symptoms: Err 99 displayed on screen or top LCD
Camera may or may not take photos
Error appears intermittently
Error appears with specific lenses but not others
Camera locks up requiring battery removal

fixSteps.title: Clean lens and body contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lens and clean the gold electrical contacts on both the lens and camera body with a microfiber cloth or pencil eraser.

fixSteps.title: Try a different memory card
fixSteps.instruction: Format a different, known-good memory card in the camera and try shooting. Card issues are a common Err 99 trigger.

fixSteps.title: Use a genuine fully-charged battery
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the battery with a genuine Canon battery at full charge. Weak or third-party batteries frequently cause Err 99.

fixSteps.title: Test without a lens
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lens and try taking a photo with the body cap off. If Err 99 disappears, the issue is lens-related.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Menu > Setup > Reset All Settings. This eliminates any software setting that might trigger the error.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest firmware for your camera model from Canon's website.

whenToCallPro: If Err 99 persists after cleaning contacts, trying different batteries, cards, and lenses, and performing a factory reset and firmware update, professional diagnostics are needed.

faq.q: Why is Err 99 so common on Canon cameras?
faq.a: Err 99 is a catch-all code used when the camera detects a problem but cannot identify it specifically. Because it covers many possible causes, it appears frequently across all Canon models.

faq.q: Does Err 99 mean my camera is broken?
faq.a: Usually not. In most cases, it is caused by dirty contacts, a bad memory card, or a weak battery. Systematic troubleshooting resolves the majority of Err 99 cases.

faq.q: Can Err 99 damage my camera if I keep shooting?
faq.a: If the error is intermittent and caused by contacts, no. However, if it is caused by a failing shutter or circuit board, continued use could worsen the issue. Get persistent Err 99 diagnosed.

title: Memory Card Door Open or Card Not Detected
shortDescription: The camera detects that the memory card door is open or no card is properly inserted.
fullDescription:
E32 appears on some Canon cameras when the memory card compartment door is open, the card is not fully inserted, or the card door switch is malfunctioning. This is a safety mechanism to prevent shooting without a card or with an insecure card connection.

On some models, this code appears as a simple warning rather than a blocking error. If 'Shoot without card' is enabled in the menu, the camera may still take photos but cannot save them.

The card door switch is a small mechanical or magnetic sensor that can wear out over time, especially on cameras used in harsh conditions.

causes.cause: Memory card not fully seated in the slot

symptoms: E32 displayed on screen
Camera does not save images despite shutter firing
Card door warning icon on display
Error persists even when card door appears closed

fixSteps.title: Close the card door securely
fixSteps.instruction: Open the card door fully, ensure the memory card is pushed in until it clicks, then close the door firmly until it latches.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the card door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the card door latch mechanism is clean and clicks firmly into place. Look for any debris preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Check the card door switch
fixSteps.instruction: With a flashlight, look inside the card slot area for a small switch or lever that is pressed when the door closes. Ensure it moves freely and is not stuck.

fixSteps.title: Disable 'Shoot without card' warning
fixSteps.instruction: As a temporary workaround, go to Menu > Shooting > Release Shutter Without Card and set to Enable. Note that images will not be saved unless a card is inserted.

whenToCallPro: If the card door switch is broken or the door latch is damaged, professional repair is needed to replace the card slot assembly.
videoSearchQuery: Canon camera E32 card door error fix

faq.q: Why does my camera show E32 with the door closed?
faq.a: The small switch inside the card slot that detects the door position may be stuck or broken. Try gently pressing it with a toothpick to see if it moves. If not, the card slot assembly needs professional repair.

faq.q: Can I ignore E32 and keep shooting?
faq.a: You can enable 'Shoot without card' in the menu, but images will not be saved unless a card is properly inserted and detected.
